feature_height.physical_meaning: Height of the channel; gap height
feature_height.definition: Vertical dimension of the lateral channel

annotation.uncertainty_note: Paper uses µm in examples, normalized to m.

feature_width.physical_meaning: Width of the channel in the z-direction
feature_width.definition: Lateral dimension of the channel perpendicular to height and length

annotation.uncertainty_note: Paper uses mm in examples, normalized to m.

feature_depth.physical_meaning: Length of the channel in the x-direction (propagation direction)
feature_depth.definition: Axial length of the channel, along which gas propagates

annotation.uncertainty_note: Paper uses mm in examples, normalized to m. This corresponds to the 'gap length' for aspect ratio calculation.

aspect_ratio.physical_meaning: Ratio of channel length (L) to channel height (H); also denoted as α

annotation.uncertainty_note: Paper uses 'a' in nomenclature (S00053) and 'α' in equation context (S00263). Definition is L/H (S00113, S00280, S00300).

hydraulic_diameter.definition: Characteristic length for a rectangular channel of height H and width W

surface_area.physical_meaning: Internal surface area of the channel available for adsorption
surface_area.definition: 2 * W * x (for a channel segment of length x)

annotation.uncertainty_note: Not explicitly defined as a single variable, but implied by the terms in adsorption equations.
Not explicitly defined as a single variable, but used as 'area WH'.

feature_volume.physical_meaning: Volume of the channel segment where reactant is present
feature_volume.definition: W * H * x_p (where x_p is penetration depth)

annotation.uncertainty_note: Not explicitly defined as a single variable, but implied by the term 'WHx_p' for reactant volume.

spatial_variable.physical_meaning: Distance from the beginning of the channel; propagation direction
spatial_variable.definition: Coordinate along the length of the channel where gas propagates

characteristic_length.physical_meaning: Hydraulic diameter of the channel, used as a characteristic length for diffusion (e.g., Knudsen diffusion)
characteristic_length.definition: Hydraulic diameter of a rectangular channel of height H and width W

diffusion_length.physical_meaning: Length scale over which gas propagates by diffusion
diffusion_length.definition: Proportional to the square root of effective diffusion coefficient and time

annotation.uncertainty_note: Not a single symbol, but an expression for diffusion length.

surface_area.physical_meaning: Internal surface area of the channel available for adsorption
surface_area.definition: 2 * W * x (for a channel segment of length x)

annotation.uncertainty_note: Not explicitly defined as a single variable, but implied by the terms in adsorption equations.

source_reference.paper_title: Modeling growth kinetics of thin films made by atomic layer deposition in lateral high-aspect-ratio structures
